MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: STEPHEN WALTERS, ESQ., ALLEN MATKINS, 415.273.7474, FOR PLAINTIFFS THE NINTH AND MISSION CORPORATION & BARKER PACIFIC GROUP INC.
PAUL RIEHLE, ESQ., SEDGWICK LLP, 415.781.7900, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT ENCORE BANK.
MIKE BUCKLEY, ESQ., REED SMITH LLP, 415.659.4761, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON TRUST COMPANY.
MATTHEW COVINGTON, ESQ., DLA PIPER LLP, 415.615.6006, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT ENCORE BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, is denied in its entirety. Defendant fails to maintain its summary judgment burden. Defendant's moving papers fail to demonstrate that the claim is time barred. Specifically, the moving papers do not show that the trustee repudiated the trust and triggered the running of the statute of limitations. The Court did not consider arguments made and evidence submitted for the first time in the reply papers or that was not included in defendant's separate statement of undisputed material facts. Judge Kahn discloses that more than fifteen years ago, and perhaps a lot longer than that, as an attorney he represented an entity or entities affiliated with the Barker Pacific Group in a lawsuit wholly unrelated to the subject of this case and that he believes that nothing about that prior representation causes him to be other than fair and impartial to all parties in this case. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
